title: Publisher Admission, Buffering, and Backpressure · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a cancellable Go publisher admission controller with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a cancellable Go publisher admission controller and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Treat a nil PublishWithContext error as local call acceptance only and separately track flow control, routing, confirmation, and stakeholder effect.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Publish Context Admission
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 7673 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; reject stale connection and channel generations; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat a nil PublishWithContext error as local call acceptance only and separately track flow control, routing, confirmation, and stakeholder effect.

Inspect the Publish Context Admission trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Blocked Unblocked Flow
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-blocked-unblocked-flow-1l2txb" that uses case 7016 to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.

At RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Blocked Unblocked Flow, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go messaging team handles case 1147 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Blocked Unblocked Flow, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A drain event proves RabbitMQ resource alarms and queue backlog have cleared. In Go, PublishWithContext is not proof that broker I/O stopped when its context ended.
